docs.title: Introduction
docs.text: gemtext is a small and simple library that provides code for parsing the hypertext markup language of the Gemini project.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
gemtext is available from pypi and can be installed with your package installer of choice.

With pip:

pip install gemtext

With uv:

uv add gemtext

docs.title: Quick start
docs.text:
The library provides a single main parsing class called Gemtext. It is initialised with a text of gemtext and the content property provides a list of lines, typed with their parsed type, each having its own relevant properties.

A very minimal parser might look like this:

import fileinput
from gemtext import Gemtext

def parse_input() -> None:
    for gem_line in Gemtext("".join(fileinput.input())).content:
        print(f"{gem_line!r}")

The library contains a simple test command line tool, which can be accessed either via the Python -m switch, or depending on your environment, via the gemtext command. For example, given this content of a file called example.gmi:

# This is a heading

## This is a sub-heading

### This is a sub-sub-heading

=> gemini://davep.gemcities.com/ Dave's test capsule

> This is a deep and meaningful quote

```
Here is some pre-formatted text.

Here's some more of that text.
```

* One
* Two
* Three

The gemtext command (or python -m gemtext) would produce:

$ gemtext example.gmi
Heading(content='This is a heading', level=1)
Paragraph(content='')
Heading(content='This is a sub-heading', level=2)
Paragraph(content='')
Heading(content='This is a sub-sub-heading', level=3)
Paragraph(content='')
Link(content="Dave's test capsule", uri='gemini://davep.gemcities.com/')
Paragraph(content='')
Quote(content='This is a deep and meaningful quote')
Paragraph(content='')
PreFormatted(content="Here is some pre-formatted text.\n\nHere's some more of that text.")
Paragraph(content='')
ListItem(content='One')
ListItem(content='Two')
ListItem(content='Three')

docs.title: gemtext
docs.text: Provides tools for working with Gemtext.

docs.title: Gemtext
docs.text:
Gemtext(text: str)

A Gemtext parser.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default str

The Gemtext content to parse.

required

docs.title: content cached property
docs.text:
content: tuple[Line, ...]

The content of the Gemtext.

This is a tuple of objects representing the parsed lines of the Gemtext content.

docs.title: __str__
docs.text:
__str__() -> str

Return the Gemtext content as a string.

docs.title: Heading
docs.text:
Heading(content: str, level: int)

Bases: Line

A heading line in Gemtext.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default str

The content of the heading.

required int

The level of the heading (1-3).

required

docs.title: level property
docs.text:
level: int

The level of the heading.

docs.title: Line
docs.text:
Line(content: str)

A single line of Gemtext.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default str

The content of the line.

required

docs.title: __repr__
docs.text:
__repr__() -> str

Return a string representation of the line.

docs.title: __str__
docs.text:
__str__() -> str

Return the content of the line as a string.
